title: JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley among U.S. banks raising dividends after stress tests
text: Major U.S. banks including Morgan Stanley and JP Morgan Chase & Co. announced dividend increases late Friday, following the Federal Reserve's stress tests earlier this week.

title: Morgan Stanley Announces 7.5 Cents Dividend Increase and Authorization of a Renewed $20 Billion Multi-Year Common Equity Share Repurchase Program
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Morgan Stanley (NYSE: MS) announced that it will increase its quarterly common stock dividend to $0.85 per share from the current $0.775 per share, beginning with the common stock dividend expected to be declared by the Firm's Board of Directors in the third quarter of 2023. In addition, the Firm's Board of Directors reauthorized a multi-year common equity share repurchase program of up to $20 billion, without a set expiration date, beginning in the third quarter of 2.

title: 3 Big Winners from Federal Reserve Bank Stress Testing
text: Stress testing is not just to ensure the safety and soundness of the banking system, but also to determine large bank regulatory capital requirements. These requirements determine how much capital banks can return to shareholders.

title: Why Dividend Investors Must Watch Bank Stocks Now
text: Markets were mixed, with tech taking a back seat on Thursday. Bank stress tests from the Federal Reserve were generally favorable.

title: US banks shrug off turmoil to ace Fed's annual health checks
text: Among the top performers were Charles Schwab Corp. and Deutsche Bank's U.S. operations, while regional lenders Citizens Financial Corp. and US Bancorp were the laggards of the pack.

title: U.S. biggest banks can weather severe recession: Fed's annual bank stress test
text: (Kitco News) - All 23 of the U.S. biggest banks are well positioned to weather a severe recession scenario and continue to provide lending to households and businesses, the Federal Reserve said in its annual bank stress test.

title: The Fed is about to reveal how safe America's biggest banks are after the crisis earlier this year
text: The Federal Reserve is set to release the results from its annual bank stress test on Wednesday after US markets close. The tests, born out of the Great Recession, are undertaken in order to determine if the nation's largest banks can withstand a severe economic downturn.

title: Broker Faces Insider Trading Probe Tied to Morgan Stanley Deals
text: Prosecutors are examining whether a securities broker and his associates traded on unlawfully obtained information about planned mergers and acquisitions.

title: David Lloyd Leisure up for sale, owner seeks £2bn, reports Sky
text: One of the best known names in the leisure world is up for sale, according to Sky. The owner of David Lloyd Leisure, one of Britain's biggest upmarket health and racquet sports chains, is preparing to listen to offers of more than £2bn, the report said.

title: Morgan Stanley trading near lows for the month in its sixth straight day of declines
text: Morgan Stanley's MS, -0.11% stock is down 0.2% to $83.46 a share in Monday trading. If it ends the session at that level on Monday, it will secure its lowest close since June 1, when it ended the session at $82.07, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

title: How are 23 Large Banks Going to Fare in 2023 Stress Test?
text: Large banks, including Goldman, Bank of America, Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan and Citigroup, will be in spotlight as the Fed releases 2023 stress test results later this week.

title: Big US banks to face Fed's annual stress tests this week
text: The Federal Reserve will release the results of this year's banking "stress tests" on Wednesday, helping assess the stability of financial instutions.

title: Big US banks to fare well in annual health checks despite spring turmoil
text: Big U.S. lenders are expected to show they have ample capital to weather any fresh turmoil in the banking sector during this week's Federal Reserve health checks, although resulting investor payouts are likely to dip slightly, analysts said.

title: Bank stocks fall again in fourth straight day of losses
text: Bank stocks are down on Thursday for the fourth straight day amid talk of higher interest rates and increased capital requirements by regulators to strengthen the banking system. Eighty-eight out of 89 components of the SPDR S&P Bank ETF KBE, -2.19% are down, while the overall index is lower by 2.4%.

text: Higher funding costs are putting pressure on net interest income and margins. Several management teams seem to have underestimated just how tough conditions might become.

title: Morgan Stanley's (MS) Inorganic Expansion Aids Amid High Costs
text: Strategic business expansion efforts to focus on WM & IM segments are likely to support Morgan Stanley (MS). Yet, rising expenses and uncertain performance of capital markets are headwinds.

title: Banks report the largest-ever quarterly sequential drop in uninsured deposits in Q1: S&P
text: Bank of America Corp. BAC, Citigroup Inc. C and Wells Fargo & Co. WFC reported drops in uninsured deposits as of March 31 as part of an overall sequential decline of 7.8% or $597.49 billion in uninsured deposits by all U.S. banks as of the end of the first quarter, S&P Global Inc. said in a research note published Thursday. JPMorgan Chase & Co. JPM reported a 1.9% increase in uninsured deposits.

title: Morgan Stanley's Investment Chief Says the Bear Market Is "Still Alive." Should You Invest?
text: Some say a new bull market is here after last week's stock market gains. But Morgan Stanley's chief investment officer Mike Wilson says earnings estimates are too high considering the economic climate -- and that could weigh further on stock performance.

title: 3 Investment Bank Stocks on the Radar Despite Industry Woes
text: A muted IB performance amid rising rates and other macroeconomic woes will keep hurting the Zacks Investment Bank industry. Yet, a decent trading business is likely to offer some support to industry players like Morgan Stanley (MS), Goldman (GS) & Evercore (EVR).

title: 10,048 Nigerians suffering from Multiple Sclerosis – Neurologist
text: About 10,048 Nigerians are suffering from Multiple Sclerosis (MS), a combined brain and spinal cord ailment, the Nigerian Society of Neurological Sciences (NSNS), has said. title: US bank stocks rebound, regional banking index hits 6-week high
text: Shares of major U.S. banks and regional lenders outperformed broader markets in morning trading on Tuesday with the KBW Regional Banking index hitting its highest since late April.

title: SCIEX launches the Intabio ZT system at ASMS 2023
text: FRAMINGHAM, Mass.–(BUSINESS WIRE)–#biopharma–At ASMS 2023, SCIEX, a global leader in life science analytical technologies, launches the Intabio ZT system, the first fully-integrated microfluidic chip-based platform combining imaged capillary isoelectric focusing (icIEF) separation and UV detection. title: Stocks Are Trying to Break Out. Morgan Stanley Expects a Big Drop.
text: The investment bank forecast a decline of 10% or more for the S&P 500, citing high valuations and risks to corporate earnings.

title: Morgan Stanley sees slump in US earnings in 2023, sharp rebound next year
text: Morgan Stanley warns of a 16% drop in profit for S&P 500 companies this year, followed by a sharp rebound in 2024 when analysts say the Federal Reserve's policy will become more accommodative.

title: US corporate earnings will slump 16% this year and that's going to derail the stock market rally, Morgan Stanley says
text: A decline in corporate earnings will stop the stock-market rally in its tracks, Morgan Stanley said. Earnings per share for the S&P 500 will fall by 16%, the team of strategists said, per Bloomberg.

title: Morgan Stanley sees an earnings wipe out ahead for Wall Street's unloved stock rally
text: Hunting for optimists the Monday after an explosive jobs and debt-ceiling fueled relief rally that sent the S&P 500 SPX, +1.45% to the edge of a bull market, is proving a little tough. That said, there's nothing horrible in the setup, with tech just a little softer as oil is up after OPEC pledged another production cut.

title: Nasdaq 100 index: Morgan Stanley sees a moment of reckoning
text: The Nasdaq 100 index continued its bullish rally as risks to the American economy eased. The tech-heavy index surged to a high of $14,557, the highest level since April last year.

title: My Top 10 High Yield Dividend Stocks For June 2023
text: I present my top 10 High Yield dividend stocks that are poised to offer strong future returns. The top 10 stocks on the watchlist offer a collective 5.08% dividend yield and have grown their dividends at a historical rate of 35.55% per year over the last five years. New stocks on the watchlist include Best Buy, Goldman Sachs, and Paychex, all of which appear undervalued based on dividend yield theory and have strong dividend growth histories.

title: Morgan Stanley's (MS) Trading, IB View Weak Amid Industry Woes
